people.name: Travis Ross
people.pain_point: ZoomInfo is a costly and outdated solution for data.
people.evidence: ZoomInfo Data Rot: A Costly, Outdated Solution... expensive, our team can help you clean them up fast.

people.name: Will Cannon
people.pain_point: ZoomInfo charges users for outdated contacts and unused credits.
people.evidence: Outdated contacts, and credits they never used.

people.name: Alex Milman-Blum
people.pain_point: ZoomInfo is too expensive and suffers from inconsistent data accuracy.
people.evidence: ZoomInfo is too expensive. Data accuracy feels inconsistent. The platform is overloaded.

people.name: Sam Holding
people.pain_point: ZoomInfo hides its pricing and makes it difficult for customers to cancel.
people.evidence: Cancel - hide pricing - hope customers give up and just let it renew.

people.name: Joseph G.
people.pain_point: Prospecting tools are expensive and provide outdated contacts that bounce.
people.evidence: The most expensive tool in your tech stack... outdated contacts, and credits they never used.

people.name: Stephen Comstock
people.pain_point: Apollo's customer support is unresponsive and overly reliant on AI.
people.evidence: Apollo's human support has taken a back seat to AI. I felt like I was communicating to a shadow down a long hallway.

people.name: M Firestone
people.pain_point: Apollo's support team is unhelpful when dealing with technical campaign questions.
people.evidence: I'm calling out the support team at Apollo.io — pay attention.

people.name: Eric Quanstrom
people.pain_point: Apollo users experience deliverability issues and sending limits.
people.evidence: Apollo killed our deliverability — mailbox warm-up, authentication, and sending limits are real constraints.

people.name: Rozana Petrovska
people.pain_point: Apollo's customer support gaslights users about their poor email deliverability.
people.evidence: Apollo's customer support is still trying to convince me that their deliverability is better than everyone else. It's just gaslighting!

people.name: Everett Berry
people.pain_point: Apollo's API limits prevent data enrichment at scale.
people.evidence: API limits which prevented enrichment at scale using Apollo's data.

people.name: Asher Chua
people.pain_point: Clay credits are too limited and the platform becomes very expensive quickly.
people.evidence: The biggest complaint I hear from new users of Clay is the fact that Clay credits are really limited and it gets really expensive.

people.name: Diana Melchor
people.pain_point: Users waste expensive Clay credits on simple actions that could be done with formulas.
people.evidence: Stop wasting Clay credits every month.

people.name: James Hanzimanolis
people.pain_point: Clay's pricing is atrocious with ~24% wrong phone data.
people.evidence: Clay's pricing is atrocious. In 1 week, I spent $800 on phone credits with ~24% being wrong.

people.name: Isaac Wood
people.pain_point: Certain Clay credits are very expensive and offer low leverage.
people.evidence: Some credits are worth it while others are very expensive and lower leverage.

people.name: Jacob Tuwiner
people.pain_point: Clay's data is expensive and users easily misuse their credits.
people.evidence: A prospect just told me using Clay is like... expensive data.

people.name: Jay Teoh
people.pain_point: Lusha provides inaccurate data and lacks accountability.
people.evidence: Lusha Data Error: Inaccurate Data and Lack of Accountability.

people.name: Chris Middleton
people.pain_point: Lusha's AI-powered lead generation is flawed, invasive, and hard to cancel.
people.evidence: Lusha claims to be a trusted B2B contact resource... hard to cancel.

people.name: Joshua Fryszer
people.pain_point: ZoomInfo, Apollo, and Lusha suffer from stale data, sticky contracts, and high bounce rates.
people.evidence: I went through 580 Reddit comments and 942 reviews — stale data and sticky contracts are the recurring complaint.

people.name: Mike Heller
people.pain_point: Clearbit is priced for larger customers and too expensive for startups.
people.evidence: Clearbit, like most established data vendors, is now priced for larger customers and often too expensive for a startup.

people.name: Jason Rosenthal
people.pain_point: ZoomInfo charges premium pricing despite 30% bad data and high bounce rates.
people.evidence: 30% of their data was bad and they're still paying ZoomInfo premium pricing.

people.name: Sunil Vali
people.pain_point: Prospecting tools like Apollo, ZoomInfo, and Clay only provide names without context or reasons to reach out.
people.evidence: A list is not a lead. Apollo, ZoomInfo and Clay sell you names, not reasons.

people.name: Adam Robinson
people.pain_point: Contact data providers are consistently too expensive and take advantage of startups.
people.evidence: Every time we switch it STILL feels too expensive.

people.name: Mitchell Keller
people.pain_point: High costs for stale funding signals.
people.evidence: Stop paying hundreds a month for stale funding signals.

people.name: Gavin Zaentz
people.pain_point: Stale data, wrong timing, wasting half the day qualifying leads.
people.evidence: Stale data, wrong timing, and half the day spent qualifying leads before you could even think about reaching out.
